picks.reason: deGrom carries a 0.81-run SIERA edge over Pérez, but Texas has zero fresh T1 leverage arms and the market already prices in deGrom’s dominance at -121. My TEX win probability is roughly 56%, a 1.2-point gap. The total estimate is 8.3 runs, 0.8 short of the 1.0-run gate.

Bibee’s SIERA is nearly a full run better than Fedde’s, but Cleveland’s bullpen has no fresh T1 arm and the offense carries a 92 wRC+ against righties. My CLE win probability is ~56%, a 1.9-point gap. The total estimate is 8.4 runs, just shy of the 8.5 line and below my 1.0-run threshold.

Coors Field side bets are excluded by method rule due to extreme variance. Suarez and Freeland both carry [sm] recent-form flags, widening true-talent bands and triggering the small-sample pass threshold. No edge survives the uncertainty premium.

Soriano is roughly 1.3 runs better than Gibson by SIERA, but Gibson’s small-sample flag (26.1 IP, 5.39 SIERA on a wide band) means the true gap collapses under expanded uncertainty. My LAA estimate is 58%, a 2.5-point gap that does not clear the 3.5-point gate. The total projects to 8.7 runs, short of the 9.5 line.

Skubal is elite (2.71 AGG xFIP, 118 Stf+), but Weathers is a legitimate above-average arm (3.38 SIERA) and the Yankees’ offense limits Detroit’s margin. The pitching gap yields only a half-point move, leaving the side well short of the 3.5-point gate.

Cameron and Jax are a dead heat by SIERA, and the Royals’ taxed bullpen (1.5 freshness score) faces a rested Tampa pen (2.25). My combined run estimate is 9.2 against an 8.0 line, but both starters carry [sm] recent flags; under the small-sample rule the wider confidence band straddles no-edge, forcing a lean only.

My combined run estimate is 9.2 against an 8.0 line, but both starters carry [sm] recent flags; under the small-sample rule the wider confidence band straddles no-edge, forcing a lean only.

Woo and Ashcraft are essentially dead-even by SIERA, and while Seattle owns a bullpen-freshness edge, the moneyline already gives the Mariners slight dog value. My win-probability estimate is 49%, offering no side edge. The total estimate is 8.2 runs, 0.7 short of the 1.0-run gate.

Nola’s ERA is results noise; his 3.82 AGG xFIP gives him a 0.65-run SIERA edge on Mikolas, but Philadelphia’s bullpen is only modestly rested and the Nats’ offense is within 10 wRC+ points. Side estimate 57.4%, a 1.3-point gap. Total estimate 9.8 runs, just 0.3 from the 9.5 line.

Yesavage and Burrows are separated by only 0.2 runs of SIERA, and while Toronto’s bullpen is fresher, Houston’s offense is eight wRC+ points better versus righties. The side is priced efficiently at roughly 60% Toronto. Total estimate is 8.6 runs, 0.1 from the 8.5 line.

Drohan’s SIERA is 0.64 runs better than Lowder’s, but Milwaukee’s moneyline already prices in most of that gap at -140. Both offenses are strong in the split, but Great American Ball Park’s 1.01 runs factor is already baked into the 9.0 line. Side edge 1.3 points; total estimate 9.2 runs.

Ohtani and Ryan own shiny ERAs, but their blended xFIP profiles (3.87 and 3.12) are merely good against two top-quartile offenses. Minnesota’s bullpen is taxed (0 of 1 T1 fresh, 1.5 score) and Target Field’s wind is carrying. I estimate 8.9 combined runs, 1.4 above the 7.5 total.

Arizona’s listed starter Mitch Bratt has no IP, xFIP, SIERA, or Stuff+ data in the feed, functioning as a TBD arm under the data-integrity rule. St. Louis is not bettable against an unpriced opponent.
picks.data_gap: Bratt lacks all predictive pitching data.

picks.reason: San Diego’s listed starter JP Sears has no IP, xFIP, SIERA, or Stuff+ data, triggering the TBD pass rule. No side or total can be priced.
picks.data_gap: Sears lacks all predictive pitching data.

picks.reason: Jump and Mahle are contact-oriented starters with below-average strike-zone dominance (Mahle Stf+ 89, Jump K-BB% 14.4%) facing lineups both above 110 wRC+ in the platoon split. Oakland’s taxed bullpen (0 of 2 T1 fresh, 1.5 score) will leak late runs, while Oracle’s short RF porch and west wind help power. I estimate 9.2 combined runs.
